heading: Recruitment activities
label: 1616b Recruitment activities
notes: Amendments1992—, substituted “Recruitment activities” for “Travel expenses for recruitment” in section catchline.Pub. L. 102–573, § 107(1)Subsec. (b). , amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “There are authorized to be appropriated $100,000 for each of the fiscal years 1990, 1991, and 1992, for the purpose of carrying out the provisions of this section.”Pub. L. 102–573, § 107(2)
amendments: Amendments1992—, substituted “Recruitment activities” for “Travel expenses for recruitment” in section catchline.Pub. L. 102–573, § 107(1)Subsec. (b). , amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “There are authorized to be appropriated $100,000 for each of the fiscal years 1990, 1991, and 1992, for the purpose of carrying out the provisions of this section.”Pub. L. 102–573, § 107(2)

children.label: a
children.text: The Secretary may reimburse health professionals seeking positions in the Service, including individuals considering entering into a contract under , and their spouses, for actual and reasonable expenses incurred in traveling to and from their places of residence to an area in which they may be assigned for the purpose of evaluating such area with respect to such assignment.section 1616a of this title

children.label: b
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Service, shall assign one individual in each area office to be responsible on a full-time basis for recruitment activities.
